items.title: Attention Is All You Need
items.author: Vaswani et al.
items.take: The paper that started the transformer revolution. If you want to understand why modern AI works the way it does, start here.

items.title: The Bitter Lesson
items.author: Rich Sutton
items.take: A short, provocative argument that general methods leveraging computation always win in the long run. Changed how I think about AI progress.

items.title: Godel, Escher, Bach
items.author: Douglas Hofstadter
items.take: A mind-bending exploration of consciousness, self-reference, and formal systems. Dense but rewarding — pairs well with the Busy Beavers series.

items.title: The Art of Doing Science and Engineering
items.author: Richard Hamming
items.take: Hamming's lectures on how to do great work. The chapter on 'You and Your Research' alone is worth the read.

items.title: A Philosophy of Software Design
items.author: John Ousterhout
items.take: The best book on managing software complexity I've read. Short, opinionated, and practical.

items.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
items.author: David Thomas & Andrew Hunt
items.take: Timeless advice on craft. I re-read sections every year and always find something new.

items.title: UV Package Manager
items.author: Astral
items.take: Blazing fast Python package manager written in Rust. I wrote a whole post about why it matters.
